channel.title: Babylon Burning
channel.description: Babylon Burning specializes in reggae music, new and old - from the rocksteady of the late 60s, through the roots and rockers era of the 70s, to the foundation dancehall sounds of the early 80s, right up to the freshest releases from Jamaica and around the world.

item.title: keep it rootikal
item.description: Featuring riddims Rootikal from Koastal Kings and Film Stone from Countree Hype... new tunes from Runkus, Roe Summerz, Marcus I, Young Kulcha, Ras-I & Kabaka Pyramid... new Melbourne-produced roots from Svaha Records and Jamaica's Alovera... some early 80s foundation dancehall from George Phang's Power House label... and the usual 70s reggae classics.
item.author: Jesse I

item.title: Featuring guest presenter Lady Love Potion
item.author: Jesse I

item.title: fruits ripe
item.description: Paying respects to the late Jamaican producer and singer Phil Pratt (born George Phillips) who passed away on February 25, with a full hour of his music... plus the beautiful new Fruits Ripe riddim from Zion I Kings, message music from the Virgin Islands, and contemporary roots reggae from Max Romeo's kids Xana and Azizzi.
item.author: Jesse I

item.title: why war
item.description: Featuring new music from Perfect Giddimani, Chezidek, Etana, Mista Savona & Mia Bloom, Joe Yorke, Marcus I, Dexta Daps, Micah Shemaiah, Vanzo, Queen Omega and nuff more... riddim juggling on the biggest riddim in dancehall right now, the WYFL riddim from DJ Mac & CrashDummy, as well as the Recovery riddim from Head Concussion... and some classic early 80s anti-war tunes.
item.author: Jesse I

item.title: late night blues
item.description: Featuring new music from Kabaka Pyramid, Protoje, Shenseea, Vybz Kartel, Buju Banton, Patoranking, Busy Signal, Capleton, Bushman, Dalwayne, YT, Marcus I and more... revisiting Stone Love's classic version of the "Give Me The Right" riddim... vintage 70s deejay tunes... and some sweet 70s roots singers.
item.author: Jesse I

item.title: mother and child
item.description: Featuring Hempress Sativa's new album "Woman"... new Queen Ifrica and I-Taweh... revisiting Pow Pow's "Overstand" riddim from 2007, and Xterminator's "Gunzalis" riddim from 1997... plus some serious anti-war tunes, some big ganja anthems, and the usual vintage classics.
item.author: Jesse I

item.title: big and serious
item.description: Featuring Jah9's new EP "Open Heart Project", more from Hempress Sativa's new album "Woman", a killer new combination from Samory I & Govana, new Imeru Tafari, some Solo Banton ahead of his Melbourne show, a run of Chant Down dubplate specials on the classic 54-46 riddim, and some foundation classics from the late 60s through to the early 80s.
item.author: Jesse I

item.title: tribute to Rupie Edwards
item.description: Paying respects to the late Jamaican singer and producer Rupie Edwards (1945-2026) with an hour of his music... plus new tunes from Hempress Sativa, Double Tiger, and Australia's own Jah Tung... and a killer new combination from Protoje & Masicka, placed with some similarly sounding productions from the late 80s and early 90s.
item.author: Jesse I

item.title: forward on to zion
item.description: Showcasing the new Protoje album "The Art of Acceptance"... some pointed lyrics from Robbie Ark on his new song "Forward On To Zion" from Spanish producer Roberto Sanchez... new tunes from Marcus Gad, Medisun, Angelica Mava and Dalwayne... some early 90s classics from the Penthouse label... and a tribute to the late Jamaican singer Ernie Smith.
item.author: Jesse I

item.title: love right, live right
item.description: Featuring some vintage 70s roots, before taking it to some 90s classics from the likes of Sizzla, Luciano and Mikey General... revisiting the Runner riddim on Builders music from 2004... and more from the new Protoje album "The Art of Acceptance".
item.author: Jesse I

item.title: hill and gully ride
item.description: Featuring the biggest new riddim in dancehall, "Hill and Gully Ride" from producer Di Genius, plus the original mento inspiration and Busy Signal's tune from 2014... new music from Runkus, Chronic Law, Jaz Elise, Capleton, Perfect Giddimani, Medisun, Imeru Tafari, Jah Izrehl, Ziggi Recado, Protoje & Pressure... revisiting the Promised Land riddim on Barry U Records from 1998... the original Junjo Lawes run on the Worries In The Dance riddim... and vintage classics from the late 60s to the early 80s.
item.author: Jesse I
